The current certs were just created (valid 2026–2036) but the task is to rotate them to new ones valid for ~1 year. The setup is clear:
- `es-http-tls` secret has `ca.crt`, `tls.crt`, `tls.key` mounted into pods
- `es-http-ca` configmap publishes the CA to clients
- `es-http-old` records old fingerprints for oracle verification

Plan: generate new CA + leaf cert (1-year validity, same SANs), update the secret with both old+new CA during transition, do a rolling restart, then update the client-facing CA configmap.

input.command:
mkdir -p /tmp/es-certs-new && cd /tmp/es-certs-new

# Generate new CA key + self-signed cert (1 year)
openssl genrsa -out ca.key 2048 2>&1
openssl req -new -x509 -key ca.key -out ca.crt \
  -days 365 \
  -subj "/CN=elastic-ca/O=elastic" \
  -extensions v3_ca \
  -addext "basicConstraints=critical,CA:TRUE" \
  -addext "subjectKeyIdentifier=hash" \
  -addext "authorityKeyIdentifier=keyid:always,issuer" 2>&1

openssl x509 -noout -text -in ca.crt | grep -E "(Not Before|Not After|Subject:|Issuer:)"

input.command:
cd /tmp/es-certs-new

# Generate new leaf key
openssl genrsa -out tls.key 2048 2>&1

# Create OpenSSL config for leaf cert with all required SANs
cat > leaf.cnf <<'EOF'
[req]
distinguished_name = req_distinguished_name
req_extensions = v3_req
prompt = no

[req_distinguished_name]
CN = es-http
O = elastic

[v3_req]
subjectAltName = @alt_names
keyUsage = digitalSignature, keyEncipherment
extendedKeyUsage = serverAuth, clientAuth

[alt_names]
DNS.1 = es-http
DNS.2 = es-http.elasticsearch
DNS.3 = es-http.elasticsearch.svc
DNS.4 = es-http.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
DNS.5 = *.es-cluster.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
DNS.6 = localhost
IP.1 = 127.0.0.1
EOF

# Generate CSR
openssl req -new -key tls.key -out tls.csr -config leaf.cnf 2>&1

# Sign with new CA (1 year)
openssl x509 -req -in tls.csr -CA ca.crt -CAkey ca.key -CAcreateserial \
  -out tls.crt -days 365 \
  -extensions v3_req -extfile leaf.cnf 2>&1

openssl x509 -noout -text -in tls.crt | grep -E "(Not Before|Not After|Subject:|Issuer:|DNS:|IP:)"

export KUBECONFIG=/mydata/karma/runs/short/r7/elasticsearch-bootstrap-to-core-20260703_084259/bundle/kubeconfig

# Get old CA for transition (concatenate old+new CA so existing connections don't break immediately)
kubectl get secret es-http-tls -n elasticsearch -o jsonpath='{.data.ca\.crt}' | base64 -d > /tmp/es-certs-new/old-ca.crt

# Build the combined CA bundle: new CA first (Elasticsearch uses first match), then old CA
cat /tmp/es-certs-new/ca.crt /tmp/es-certs-new/old-ca.crt > /tmp/es-certs-new/ca-bundle.crt

echo "=== CA bundle (new + old) ==="
grep "BEGIN CERTIFICATE" /tmp/es-certs-new/ca-bundle.crt | wc -l
echo "certificates in bundle"
input.description: Build combined CA bundle (new + old) for zero-downtime transition

input.command:
export KUBECONFIG=/mydata/karma/runs/short/r7/elasticsearch-bootstrap-to-core-20260703_084259/bundle/kubeconfig

# Update the es-http-tls secret with new certs
# ca.crt = combined bundle (new CA + old CA for transition)
# tls.crt = new leaf cert signed by new CA
# tls.key = new leaf key
kubectl create secret generic es-http-tls \
  --namespace elasticsearch \
  --from-file=ca.crt=/tmp/es-certs-new/ca-bundle.crt \
  --from-file=tls.crt=/tmp/es-certs-new/tls.crt \
  --from-file=tls.key=/tmp/es-certs-new/tls.key \
  --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f - 2>&1
input.description: Update es-http-tls secret with new leaf cert and combined CA bundle
